questions.stem: Which estimating technique uses the cost of a previous similar project?
questions.explanation: Analogous estimating uses historical data from similar projects and is commonly used early in project planning.

questions.stem: Which estimating technique calculates cost based on unit rates such as cost per square foot?
questions.explanation: Parametric estimating uses statistical relationships such as cost per unit.

questions.stem: Which estimating method starts with detailed estimates for each work package?
questions.explanation: Bottom up estimating estimates costs at the lowest level of the WBS and sums them upward.

questions.stem: Which estimating method uses optimistic, pessimistic, and most likely estimates?
questions.explanation: Three point estimating calculates an average estimate using optimistic, pessimistic, and most likely values.

questions.stem: When is analogous estimating most commonly used?
choices: Early in the project when information is limited
questions.explanation: Analogous estimating is fast but less accurate and is used when little detailed information exists.

questions.stem: Which estimate type has the largest range of accuracy?
questions.explanation: ROM estimates have the widest range of accuracy typically from negative 25 percent to plus 75 percent.

questions.stem: Which estimate type is used when detailed specifications are available?
questions.explanation: Definitive estimates use detailed project information and have much smaller accuracy ranges.
